goals.g1: How should collect-all-matches get its candidate occurrences: extend find_core's contract, keep it fully separate, or merge?

c1.statement: find_core is used today only by dispatch.py for single-winner rule dispatch

c2.statement: find_core's driver loop removes mapped A/B nodes each round (progressive elimination) and keeps only the single best-scoring embedding per round

c3.statement: dispatch's verified correctness rests on 720 permutations of a 6-rule sample + 20 random shuffles of 36 add_init rules, all against find_core's CURRENT contract

c4.statement: Compound Match Resolution's collect-all-matches step needs every occurrence of every rule's core across the graph, including overlapping occurrences

c5.statement: the final acceptance test (match_all's consistent()+crossing_ok()) is independent of how a candidate seed was proposed - a bad or redundant seed just fails to produce a binding, it cannot produce a false-positive match

c6.statement: _grow_from is a reusable primitive (seed pair -> single lockstep embedding) that find_core's driver loop calls repeatedly; it does not itself do progressive elimination

c7.statement: option A (mutate find_core's own contract in place to also enumerate) risks silently changing dispatch's verified behavior since dispatch calls find_core directly and reads subgraphs[0]
c7.negation_note: selftest.py: add_init rules=36 checks=16 fires=16 mismatches=0 PASS; c9 touches neither find_core's body nor dispatch.py, so the verified contract is structurally unreachable by this change

c8.statement: option B (fully separate brute-force match_all-only path with no core pruning) discards the grouping and fingerprint-gate performance work already verified for the single-match case

c9.statement: a merged design: leave find_core's driver loop and dispatch.py call site untouched, add a sibling driver in core_finder.py (e.g. find_all_cores) that reuses _grow_from but does not remove mapped nodes between seed attempts, collecting every embedding at/above min_ratio including overlapping ones

derived_from.rule: shared-primitive merge: co-locate in same module and mechanism, do not alter or duplicate the verified single-winner contract

c10.statement: DECISION: implement find_all_cores(A_edges, B_edges, min_ratio) in core_finder.py as a sibling of find_core, sharing _grow_from, iterating every (a0,b0) seed pair without removing matched nodes from the pools between attempts, returning every accepted embedding (matched_edges, node_map) at/above min_ratio -- overlapping embeddings included, not deduplicated. dispatch.py and find_core remain untouched.

derived_from.rule: restate c9 as the committed answer statement

ans.statement: merge: add find_all_cores sibling in core_finder.py reusing _grow_from, leave find_core/dispatch.py untouched

k1.method: ran grouping/selftest.py unmodified against current core_finder.py/dispatch.py (no edits made yet) as a baseline, confirmed merge design (c9) adds a new function without editing find_core or dispatch.py's call site
k1.result: selftest.py: add_init rules=36 checks=16 fires=16 mismatches=0 PASS; c9 touches neither find_core's body nor dispatch.py, so the verified contract is structurally unreachable by this change

k2.method: re-verify the baseline test suite passes with zero code changes made (sanity that 'untouched' claim is currently true, not just planned)
k2.result: PASS, 0 mismatches, confirms starting point before any find_all_cores code is written

committed.statement: DECISION: implement find_all_cores(A_edges, B_edges, min_ratio) in core_finder.py as a sibling of find_core, sharing _grow_from, iterating every (a0,b0) seed pair without removing matched nodes from the pools between attempts, returning every accepted embedding (matched_edges, node_map) at/above min_ratio -- overlapping embeddings included, not deduplicated. dispatch.py and find_core remain untouched.
